title: Samsung PM1763 PCIe Gen6 Enterprise SSD in Production
gist: Samsung has begun mass production of the PM1763, its first PCIe Gen6 enterprise SSD, hitting 28,400 MB/s reads with 9th-gen V-NAND and a 4nm controller, signaling that AI workloads are driving the PCIe Gen6 server transition alongside CPUs like NVIDIA Vera and AMD EPYC Venice.

insights.claim: The PCIe Gen6 server transition is beginning, gated by CPU support arriving with hyperscale CPUs, NVIDIA Vera, and the upcoming AMD EPYC Venice.
insights.speaker: Cliff Robinson
insights.so_what: A platform inflection is underway; component vendors that ship Gen6-native parts early capture the AI server refresh cycle.
insights.quote: We are just starting the transition to PCIe Gen6 systems with some hyper-scale CPUs, NVIDIA Vera, and soon AMD EPYC Venice launching with support.

insights.claim: AI, not video or file workloads, is now the primary driver of PCIe Gen6 SSD adoption, benchmarked by the ability to transfer a 40GB large language model in about 1.4 seconds.
insights.speaker: Cliff Robinson
insights.so_what: SSD demand is increasingly tied to AI model-loading latency, reframing enterprise storage as an accelerator-feeding component.
insights.quote: AI is driving PCIe Gen6 SSD adoption

insights.claim: Samsung's PM1763 uses 9th-generation V-NAND and a newly developed 4nm controller to deliver up to 28,400 MB/s sequential reads and 21,900 MB/s writes in the 16TB configuration.
insights.speaker: Cliff Robinson
insights.so_what: Concrete Gen6 performance bar for enterprise SSD competitors (Solidigm, SK Hynix, Micron) to match.
insights.quote: sequential read speeds up to 28,400 MB/s while writes hit 21,900 MB/s in the 16TB configuration

insights.claim: The PM1763 delivers more than double the performance of Samsung's previous PM1753 model.
insights.speaker: Cliff Robinson
insights.so_what: Generational doubling justifies AI-server refresh spend on storage.
insights.quote: This represents more than double the performance of the previous PM1753 model.

themes.theme: Liquid cooling and AI rack design

insights.claim: The PM1763 is optimized for liquid cooling, a differentiator versus most current enterprise SSDs, because newer AI servers will be fully liquid-cooled to remove fans from the compute rack.
insights.speaker: Cliff Robinson
insights.so_what: Liquid-coolable SSD orientation becomes a design requirement for AI racks, favoring vendors that adapt physical drive designs.
insights.quote: Newer AI servers will be completely liquid-cooled to move fans out of the compute rack, and so the SSDs must also be oriented so they can be liquid-cooled.
insights.locator: Samsung's new drive is also said to be optimized for liquid cooling

insights.claim: The PM1763 supports post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithms and TEE Device Interface Security Protocol (TDISP) for securing data pathways in virtualized environments, reflecting long deployment lifespans.
insights.speaker: Cliff Robinson
insights.so_what: PQC is becoming a checkbox in enterprise storage procurement as buyers hedge against future quantum threats.
insights.quote: they now support post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithms designed to protect against future quantum computing threats
insights.locator: Since these drives will be deployed for some time

insights.claim: Samsung is launching the PM1763 in 4TB, 8TB, and 16TB capacities aimed at high-performance in-server local storage rather than the high-capacity market, mirroring prior Gen4 and Gen5 NVMe transitions.
insights.speaker: Cliff Robinson
insights.so_what: New PCIe generations debut at performance-tier capacities first; high-capacity Gen6 parts follow later in the cycle.
insights.quote: Instead of being focused at the high-capacity market, the Samsung PM1763 ships in 4TB, 8TB, and 16TB capacities.
